_comment: Autonomous post+repost decision-maker (src/triggers/ai_manager.py). Each Claude call now produces a BATCH of up to posts_per_batch posts instead of just one -- only the first is fired immediately, the rest are queued in state and drained one per subsequent run (natural hourly-cron spacing), so total posts/day (target ~10-14) can be much higher than the Claude call cadence itself (~6-7 calls/day, kept low deliberately to stay well within config/claude_budget.json's cap even on Sonnet 5 at full post-intro pricing). Since a call costs money regardless of outcome, the prompt now pushes Claude to find at least one genuinely worth-it post per call rather than defaulting to 'no action' -- combined with posts_per_batch raised to 3, this squeezes more real output out of the same (few) calls. wants_extras (Claude's own per-post call, nudged by extras_every_n_posts) decides whether a given post gets an image/link at all -- roughly 1 in extras_every_n posts, the rest post as genuine plain text (no image attempt, no link attempt), which is also by far the cheapest post shape. When a post does want extras and has no image available (OPENAI_API_KEY unset, or generation failed), the link fallback prefers the real source URL of whichever news article the post is actually based on (Claude's news_index) -- fallback_link_url (here: the public Telegram channel) is only used when the post isn't based on one specific article. Reposts are deliberately kept sparse (max_reposts_per_day: 3, max_reposts_per_call: 1) so the profile reads mostly as our own original posts rather than a mixed feed.
